快速总结：这是艰难的一年，但我们的第一季比赛已经锁定，比计划的要多，硬件锁定，操作系统几乎就绪，下个月开始批量生产第一批游戏。
此外，当重大事件发生时，我们会按照注册顺序，首先给PlayDate邮件列表上的人发电子邮件。所以，如果我们可以的话：
尽管2020年我们非常非常努力地试图击败我们-从我们的工厂关闭了几个月，到我们所有人都在家里学习一起工作-我们从未停止过在PlayDate上的工作。最后，我们在明亮的黄色隧道的尽头看到了一丝曙光。
各位，这是一个令人惊叹的阵容。还有一个额外的好消息：我们利用额外的时间从更多的开发商那里委托制作了更多的第一季游戏。很快，在一个特别的视频更新中，我们将揭晓第一季每一场比赛的名字！请期待。
提醒一下，第一季包含在您的游戏日期中。没有额外的费用。
我们已经对曲柄进行了微调。我们已经调整了棋盘。我们已经解决了在早期开发人员预览单元中发现的一些潜在的怪癖。播放日期已锁定，可以生产了。(除非我们再次更改。)。
此外，经过三个月的文书工作，多天的疲惫旅行，以及两周的强制隔离，我们在美国的制造专家史蒂文刚刚抵达马来西亚。下周他将到我们工厂监督大批量生产，确保一切都完美无缺。
有了Wi-Fi和蓝牙，PlayDate必须经过彻底的测试，以确保它在无线电限制内运行。令我们欣慰的是，截至7月，PlayDate完全通过了FCC认证(对于好奇的人来说，ID为2AR29-PDU01)，并为日本获得了MIC认证，为欧洲获得了CE认证。
我不知道是否有人特别在意，但是我们的PlayDate包装已经做好并打印好了。我们真的很享受，在封面上同时使用了DeBoss和Spot光泽！)。这里是：
对于开发人员，我们一直在马不停蹄地开发我们的SDK。这包括添加更好的冲突检测、基于Web的字体制作工具、Lua 5.4更快的Lua性能，以及我们的Lua和CAPI之间更好的奇偶校验。
但是，SDK最疯狂的消息是什么呢？我们制作了Panic的第一款Windows应用程序！除了Mac之外，我们的PlayDate模拟器现在可以在Windows和Linux上运行。
我们仍然计划在未来免费向公众发布SDK。如果感兴趣，请确保您在我们的开发人员邮件列表中。
我们向全球所有技能水平的开发人员交付了数百个Playdate单元，从业界资深人士到编写第一行代码的像素艺术家。我们还设立了开发者论坛进行支持和指导，与开发者产生了非常有趣的不和。
以下是准备分享的Developer Preview爱好者的几个可爱的亮点：
记住，每一个玩伴都是一个开发工具包。如果你想制作自己的游戏，你不需要任何特殊的硬件！第三方游戏可以直接安装在PlayDate上。他们不需要通过我们。
好的，听起来不错，但是我们知道您只有一个问题：什么时候可以订货？
我们不想拿任何人的钱，直到Playdate 100%完成并准备好走。
但与此同时，我们知道你们中的许多人想要“锁定”和保留你们的PlayDate，而你们真的不喜欢处于不确定的状态。这完全说得通。
这不是我们的计划。我们不希望PlayDate永远“卖光”。这个想法是分批安排玩耍日期，并进行开放订单-每个想要一个的人都可以订购一个。
然后，您的Playdate将根据您订购的时间和您所处的批次到达。
如果您改变主意，您可以在订单发货前随时取消订单。
嗯，我们已经正式订购了20,000个第一批的玩伴，我们认为这应该足以满足最初的需求。我们希望如此。(我们还祈祷我们没有过分夸张，因为那会相当尴尬。不管怎么说。)。
除了第一批外，我们工厂每个工作日都会安排玩耍。但他们每天只能做这么多。
当您点餐时，我们会尽力告诉您您的批次，以及您的玩耍日期应该在什么时候到达。
我们是认真的。感谢您对PlayDate的耐心和兴趣。我们很高兴你还对此感到兴奋，因为我们也是！我们知道等待从来都不是一件有趣的事，但是你的等待就要结束了。
要确切知道这是何时发生的-以及订单何时开始-请注册！
note_en:
Quick summary: a tough year, but our Season One games are locked, with more games than planned, the hardware is locked, the OS is almost there, and mass production for batch one starts next month.
    Also, when big things happen, we’ll e-mail people on the Playdate Mailing List first, in signup order. So, if we may:
      Despite 2020 trying very very hard to defeat us — from our factory shutting down for many months, to all of us learning to work together from home — we’ve never stopped work on Playdate. And, at last, we’re seeing a light at the end of the bright yellow tunnel.
  And folks, it’s an amazing lineup. There’s bonus great news too: we used our extra time to commission  even more Season One games from more developers. Soon, in a special video update, we’ll reveal the names of each of the Season One games! Please look forward to it.
    As a reminder, Season One is included with your Playdate. There’s no extra charge.
    We’ve fine-tuned the crank. We’ve tweaked the boards. We’ve addressed some potential quirks found in early Developer Preview units. Playdate is locked and ready for production. (Unless we change it again.)
    Also, after three months of paperwork, many days of tenuous travel, and a two week mandatory quarantine, our USA-based manufacturing specialist Steven has just made it to Malaysia. Next week he’ll be at our factory to supervise mass production and make sure everything is perfect.
    With both Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, Playdate had to be thoroughly tested to make sure it operates within radio limits. We’re relieved to note that as of July, Playdate is fully FCC certified (ID 2AR29-PDU01 for the curious) and is MIC certified for Japan and CE certified for Europe.
      I don’t know if anyone particularly cares, but our Playdate packaging is done and printed. We’re really living it up, using both deboss  and spot gloss on the cover!) Here it is:
        For developers, we’ve been working non-stop on our SDK. This includes adding better collision detection, a web-based font making tool, faster Lua performance with Lua 5.4, and greater parity between our Lua and C APIs.
    But, the wildest SDK news? We’ve made Panic’s first Windows app! In addition to the Mac, our Playdate Simulator is now up and running on both Windows and Linux.
  We still plan to release the SDK to the public, for free, in the future. Make sure you’re on our  developer mailing list if interested.
    We delivered hundreds of Playdate units to developers of all skill levels all around the globe, from industry veterans to pixel artists writing their first lines of code. We also set up a Developer Forum for support and guidance, and a very fun developer Discord.
  Here’s a few lovely highlights from Developer Preview folks who were ready to share:
          And remember,  every Playdate is a devkit. You won’t need any special hardware if you want to make your own games! And  third-party games can be directly installed on a Playdate. They won’t need to go through us.
  Ok, that all sounds great, but we know you have just one question: when can you order?
  We don’t want to take anyone’s money until Playdate is 100% done and ready to go.
  But at the same time, we know many of you want your Playdate “locked in” and reserved, and you really don’t enjoy being in limbo. And that makes total sense.
      That’s not the plan. We don’t want Playdate to ever be “sold out”. The idea is to make Playdates in batches and do an  open order — everybody who wants one can order one.
  Then, your Playdate will arrive based on when you ordered, and what batch you’re in.
  If you change your mind, you'll be able to cancel your order at any time before it ships.
    Well, we’ve officially ordered 20,000 Playdates for batch one, which we think should be enough to meet initial demand. We hope. (We also pray we didn’t overshoot it wildly, because that would be pretty embarrassing. Anyway.)
    Beyond that first batch, our factory will be making Playdates every weekday. But they can only make so many each day.
  When you order, we’ll do our best to tell you what batch you’re in, and when your Playdate should arrive.
    We really mean it. Thanks for your patience and interest in Playdate. We’re so glad you’re still excited about it, because we are too! We know it’s never fun to wait, but your waiting is almost over.
           To know exactly when that’s happening — and when orders are starting —  sign up!

note:
除了在关键的总统竞选中投票外，一些美国人下周将决定是否让真菌再次变得伟大。裸盖菇素-你可能知道的神奇蘑菇-曾经只是嬉皮士生动梦想的东西，现在已经在美国的四个城市以及加拿大和葡萄牙等国被合法化(而在荷兰，它们以“魔法松露”的形式是完全合法的)。11月3日，俄勒冈州和华盛顿特区可能会加入蘑菇运动。
这个国家的首都只是想使毒品合法化。俄勒冈州更进一步：它建议将其合法化用于医疗目的，并建立一个基础设施，由训练有素的专业人员在临床环境中管理蘑菇。尽管迷幻专家一致认为裸盖菇素可以帮助克服精神健康问题，但事实证明，这些框架更具争议性，因为声称这些措施将遏制毒品逮捕和监禁。
裸盖菇素目前被政府归类为迷幻药物，与LSD和MDMA(“Molly”)并列。约翰·霍普金斯大学(Johns Hopkins University)助理教授、该机构迷幻与意识研究中心成员阿尔伯特·加西亚-罗密欧(Albert Garcia-Romeu)表示，旅行中的用户经常看到移动的形状、鲜艳的颜色和物体的扭曲-不要与幻觉或发现不存在的物体混淆。他说，这更像是在地毯上看到一个图案，它“像海洋一样移动、挥动或闪烁”。
约翰·霍普金斯中心是世界上主要的迷幻研究机构之一，与纽约大学、伦敦帝国理工学院和苏黎世大学一起，所有这些都重新点燃了裸盖菇素的研究。1970年，在美国通过了《受控物质法案》，将蘑菇列为附表一物质，收紧了研究法规，裸盖菇素的研究实际上停止了。这些大学发现，人们娱乐时享受的蘑菇可以帮助为包括抑郁、焦虑、上瘾和临终痛苦在内的疾病提供持久的补救措施。加西亚-罗密欧说：“在某一天给某人服用药物，然后让他们继续以一种持久的方式获得好处，这是闻所未闻的，特别是在一些更难治疗的精神健康问题上。”
在华盛顿特区，政府雇员梅丽莎·拉瓦萨尼(Melissa Lavasani)在用尽了所有其他治疗产后抑郁症和自杀念头的选择后，第一手发现了蘑菇对健康的好处。有了更大剂量的蘑菇和其他诱导剂，或植物性精神活性物质，她能够重温过去的创伤，“并正面应对”。同样，加西亚-罗密欧也采访过一些人，他们的性欲经历“就像是他们一生中做过的所有让他们后悔的糟糕事情的亮点卷轴，”他说。“经历这一切既不好玩也不愉快，但在某种程度上，这有助于他们放手，原谅自己。”
这一个人发现促使在家中开始种植蘑菇的拉瓦萨尼提出了第81号倡议：2020年诱生植物和真菌政策法案，该法案将使蘑菇和其他诱因合法化，如阿雅瓦斯卡、佩奥特和圣佩德罗仙人掌。它不会使这些物质合法化；相反，它们将成为警方的最低优先事项，司法部长也不会起诉任何种植或使用这些物质的人。裸盖菇素已经在丹佛、安娜堡、奥克兰和圣克鲁斯合法化-拉瓦萨尼希望这项措施获得通过，以便在“更保守”的东海岸发起一场运动。“我们不希望闸门为迷幻使用而打开，”她说。“我们希望这是一个深思熟虑的过程。”
她认为这是一项警察改革措施，以“结束部分毒品战争”。加西亚-罗密欧说，虽然这一论点支持大麻合法化，但这是迷幻剂的“一种奇怪的红鲱鱼”，因为它们主要由白人使用，使用和持有的逮捕率并不是相对较高。尽管如此，拉瓦萨尼认为，如果这已经是警方的低优先级，这是一个使法律现代化以反映这一点的机会。她对这项措施的两次内部民调感到鼓舞，预计下周将有70%的人投赞成票。
俄勒冈州的做法截然不同。俄勒冈州是首批大麻合法化的州之一，旨在通过措施109使神奇蘑菇的医疗用途合法化，该措施名为裸盖菇素蘑菇服务计划倡议，已得到俄勒冈州民主党的支持。如果获得通过，它将只允许在专门设立的医疗诊所给药，这些诊所只由有执照的个人给药。在家里种植或带蘑菇仍然是不被允许的。它们将严格用于医疗用途，文本明确表示，原因是俄勒冈州精神健康疾病的高度流行，州长凯特·布朗(Kate Brown)在2018年宣布俄勒冈州为公共卫生危机。
一份71页的文件详细说明了如果该法律获得通过将采取的行动。将有两年的开发期，在此期间，州长将任命俄勒冈州裸盖菇素董事会，由俄勒冈州卫生局(OHA)监督。该委员会将“确保该计划最大限度地提高安全性，对所有将从裸盖菇素治疗中受益的人来说，都是负担得起的、可获得的和公平的，”YES on 109的竞选经理山姆·查普曼(Sam Chapman)说。直到2023年1月，该委员会才开始接受制造商、诊所运营商和药品管理员的申请。然后，它将根据待定的测试标准分发许可证。
加西亚-罗密欧对这项提议表示担忧。“我认为他们可能是本末倒置，”他说。他预计，不合格的所谓“萨满和生活教练”可能会申请成为药品管理员，当涉及到强大的物质和脆弱的人时，这是一个真正的危险。查普曼证实，管理人员将不需要是有执照的医生，在这一点上，培训和认证要求将被决定。美国精神病学协会和俄勒冈州精神病医生协会都发表了反对这项措施的声明，呼应了这些论点，并对人们认为的尽职调查不足发表了评论。“OPA同意FDA的观点，早期的有限试验已经显示出希望，”OPA的声明中写道。这一状况并不能确定这种治疗的安全性和有效性，它只是确定了进一步研究这种治疗的过程。
虽然约翰·霍普金斯大学的长期目标是允许蘑菇用于医疗，但加西亚-罗密欧担心失败的FIRST项目可能会对该运动的进展产生永久性影响。(他也不推荐像俄勒冈州的大麻药房那样的药房模式，因为裸盖菇素的性质更强，而且它更有可能发生意想不到的反应。)。他建议研究型大学收集和发布他们的数据，获得FDA的批准，并在国家层面实施基础设施，这一过程需要更长的时间。尽管如此，他承认许多迫切需要心理健康治疗的人几乎没有时间可以浪费。
另一方面，拉瓦萨尼赞扬了俄勒冈州她认为的安全模式，并表示她“真的支持”这项措施的通过。“我是一个普通人，我以一种没有多少人谈论的正常方式奋斗，”她说。“我从里面出来了，我用迷幻剂把它弄出来了。那好吧。这对每个人来说都应该没什么问题。“
note_en:
As well as casting votes in a pivotal presidential race, some Americans will next week decide whether or not to make fungus great again. Psilocybin—magic mushrooms, as you might know them—which once was just the stuff of vivid hippie dreams, already has been decriminalized in four U.S. cities, as well as countries such as Canada and Portugal (while in Holland, they’re fully legal in “magic truffle” form). On November 3, Oregon and Washington, D.C., may join the mushroom movement.
   The nation’s capital simply wants to decriminalize the drug. Oregon goes a step further: It proposes legalizing it for medical purposes and establishing an infrastructure for administering mushrooms in clinical settings by trained professionals. Though psychedelic experts agree psilocybin can help overcome mental health issues, these frameworks are proving more contentious, as are claims that the measures will curb drug arrests and incarceration.
 Psilocybin is currently categorized by the government as a psychedelic drug, along with LSD and MDMA (“Molly”). Users on trips often see moving shapes, vivid colors, and distortion of objects—not to be confused with hallucinations, or spotting objects that aren’t there, says Albert Garcia-Romeu, assistant professor at Johns Hopkins University, and a member of that institution’s Center for Psychedelic and Consciousness Research. It’s more akin to seeing a pattern on a carpet that’s “moving or waving or shimmering like the ocean,” he says.
  That  Johns Hopkins center is one of the primary psychedelic research institutes in the world, along with NYU, Imperial College London, and the University of Zurich, all of which are reigniting psilocybin research, after it effectively stopped in 1970, after the passage of the Controlled Substance Act in the U.S., which made mushrooms a Schedule I substance, tightening research regulations. The universities are finding that the same shrooms people enjoy recreationally can help provide  enduring remedies for illnesses including depression, anxiety, addiction, and end-of-life distress. “It’s kind of unheard of to give somebody a drug on one day, and then for them to continue to reap the benefits in a long-lasting way,” Garcia-Romeu says, “particularly with some of these more intractable mental health conditions.”
 In Washington, D.C., government employee Melissa Lavasani found firsthand the health benefits of mushrooms, after exhausting all other treatment options for her postpartum depression and suicidal ideation. With larger doses of shrooms and other entheogens, or plant-based psychoactive substances, she was able to revisit past trauma “and deal with it head-on.” Similarly, Garcia-Romeu has talked to people whose entheogen experiences were “like a highlight reel of all the shitty things [they] ever did in their life that they regretted,” he says. “Going through that was not fun or pleasant, but in a way, it was helpful for them to let go and to forgive themselves.”
 The personal discovery led Lavasani, who’d started growing shrooms at home, to propose Initiative 81: Entheogenic Plant and Fungus Policy Act of 2020, which would decriminalize mushrooms and other entheogens, such as ayahuasca, peyote, and San Pedro cactus. It would not legalize these substances; rather, they’d become lowest in priority for police, and the attorney general would not prosecute anyone caught growing or using them. Psilocybin is already decriminalized in Denver, Ann Arbor, Oakland, and Santa Cruz—and Lavasani wants this measure to pass in order to start a movement on the “more buttoned-up” East Coast. “We don’t want the floodgates to be open for psychedelic use,” she says. “We want this to be a thoughtful process.”
 She views it as a police reform measure, to  “end part of the War on Drugs.” While this argument holds up for decriminalizing marijuana, Garcia-Romeu says, it’s “kind of a weird red herring” with psychedelics, because they’re  mainly used by white people, and arrest rates for use and possession aren’t comparatively high. Still, Lavasani argues that if it’s already a low police priority, this is a chance to modernize the law to reflect it. She’s encouraged by two internal polls on the measure and is expecting 70% to vote in favor next week.
  Oregon’s approach is very different. One of the first states to legalize marijuana, Oregon aims to legalize the medical use of magic mushrooms with Measure 109, the Psilocybin Mushroom Services Program Initiative, which has been endorsed by the  Democratic Party of Oregon. If passed, it would allow administration of the drug only at specially established health clinics, administered only by licensed individuals. Growing or taking shrooms at home would still not be permitted. They would strictly be for medical use, with the text stating explicitly that the reason is Oregon’s high prevalence of mental health illness, which Governor Kate Brown declared a public health crisis in 2018.
  A  71-page document details the course of action if the law were to pass. There would be a two-year development period, during which the governor would appoint an Oregon Psilocybin Board, overseen by the Oregon Health Authority (OHA). That board would “ensure that the program maximizes safety and is affordable, accessible, and equitable for all who stand to benefit from psilocybin therapy,” says Sam Chapman, campaign manager for  Yes on 109. Only in January 2023 would the board start receiving applications for manufacturers, clinic operators, and drug administrators. It would then distribute licenses based on to-be-determined testing standards.
 Garcia-Romeu has concerns about the proposal. “I think they may be putting the cart before the horse,” he says. He foresees that unqualified so-called “shamans and life coaches” might apply as drug administrators, which is a real danger when powerful substances and vulnerable people are involved. Chapman confirms that administrators won’t need to be licensed physicians, and at this point, training and certification requirements are to be decided. The American Psychiatric Association and the Oregon Psychiatric Physicians Association both released statements opposing the measure, echoing those arguments, and also commenting on a perceived lack of due diligence. “The OPPA agrees with the FDA, that early limited trials have shown promise,”  the OPPA’s statement reads. “This status does not establish the safety and efficacy of this treatment, it merely establishes the process by which to further study the treatment.”
 While Johns Hopkins’s long-term aim is to allow mushrooms for medical treatment, Garcia-Romeu worries a failed first project could have permanent effects on the progress of the movement. (He also does not recommend a dispensary model, such as Oregon has for cannabis, because of the stronger nature of psilocybin and its greater potential for unexpected reactions.) He recommends a longer process of research universities collecting and releasing their data, achieving FDA approval, and implementing an infrastructure on a national level. Still, he recognizes that many people who urgently need mental health treatment have little time to waste.
 Lavasani, on the other hand, commends Oregon for what she views as a safe model, and says she’s “really rooting” for the measure to pass. “I’m a normal person, and I struggled in a normal way that not very many people were talking about,” she says. “I came out of it, and I used psychedelics to come out of it. That’s okay. And that should be okay for everybody.”

我听说H-1B彩票的随机性正在消失。这对我们的初创公司为我们的联合创始人之一获得H-1B签证的能力意味着什么？
本周移民局发生了很多事情(和往常一样！)。首先，对绿卡申请者来说是个好消息：2020年11月的签证公告与10月份相比没有变化，当时在印度和中国出生的个人申请身份调整的日期明显加快。
关于H-1B抽签：负责监管美国公民和移民服务局(USCIS)的国土安全部(DHS)本周提出了一项规则，结束随机的H-1B抽签；取而代之的是，USCIS将决定谁可以根据最高工资申请H-1B签证。国土安全部表示，这一变化将“激励雇主提供更高的工资”。
每年发放的H-1B签证数量上限为8.5万个。目前，当H-1B的需求超过年度供应时，自2013年以来一直是这种情况，移民局使用电子随机抽奖来确定谁可以申请H-1B。今年3月，赞助公司首次以电子方式登记了每个H-1B候选人参加彩票。

   I heard the randomness of the H-1B lottery is going away. What will this mean for our startup’s ability to get an H-1B visa for one of our co-founders?
   Lots going on in immigration this week (as usual!). First, good news for green card applicants: the  November 2020 Visa Bulletin did not change from October, when the dates for filing for Adjustment of Status sped up significantly for individuals born in India and China.
 About the H-1B lottery: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), which oversees U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS), this week proposed  a rule that ends the random H-1B lottery; instead, USCIS will determine who can apply for an  H-1B visa based on the highest salary. DHS says this change will “incentivize employers to offer higher wages.”
 The number of H-1B visas issued each year is capped at 85,000. Currently, when demand for H-1Bs outstrips the annual supply, which has been the case since 2013, USCIS uses an electronic random  lottery to determine who can apply for an H-1B. For the first time this year, sponsoring companies electronically registered each H-1B candidate for the lottery in March.
title: 亲爱的索菲：特朗普获胜会取消H-1B签证抽奖吗？
